metadata.name: production-security-waivers
metadata.note: These entries explain non-production examples and required host-level Kubernetes workloads. They do not waive the formal NetworkPolicy-capable CNI blocker or any production Secret/TLS requirement.

local_kafka_plaintext.reason: Local docker-compose bootstrap keeps PLAINTEXT listeners for developer-only control-plane tests; production Kafka is validated separately through SASL_SSL/TLS live evidence.
Module docker-compose fixture is not part of the production K8s release package and is used only for local CEP job development.
Legacy module-level Kubernetes sample is outside the current production release root; production manifests use the secured Kafka profile and image lock evidence.
Legacy module-level Kubernetes sample is outside the current production release root; production manifests use the secured Kafka profile and image lock evidence.
Module docker-compose fixture is not part of the production K8s release package and is used only for local alert-generator job development.
Module docker-compose fixture is not part of the production K8s release package and is used only for local session job development.
Module docker-compose fixture is not part of the production K8s release package and is used only for local PCAP index job development.
Module docker-compose fixture is not part of the production K8s release package and is used only for local rule job development.

raw_secret_manifests.reason: This file contains placeholder `<GENERATED_BASE64>` values and documented kubectl generation commands; live production credentials are reconciled through ExternalSecrets and verified by production ExternalSecret reconciliation evidence.

privileged_containers.reason: kube-proxy requires privileged node networking access as part of the Kubernetes data plane and is not an application workload.
Probe Agent requires host packet capture capabilities for full-traffic collection; this exception must remain isolated to the DaemonSet and covered by NetworkPolicy/CNI and mTLS controls.

host_namespace_workloads.reason: Flannel requires host networking as the current cluster CNI; this does not close the NetworkPolicy enforcement blocker because Flannel is not policy-capable.
kube-proxy requires host networking as part of the Kubernetes node data plane and is not an application workload.
MetalLB speaker requires host networking to announce service addresses on the local network; it is an infrastructure component outside the application namespace.
Probe Agent requires hostNetwork and hostPID for full-traffic capture and interface visibility; this exception must remain limited to probe capture nodes and does not waive CNI enforcement.
